message:
feat(test): parallelize dolt storage tests for 3.5x speedup (bd-5ua)

Add t.Parallel() to setupTestStore and openTestDoltBranch helpers so
all branch-isolated tests run concurrently. Each test gets its own Dolt
connection on a unique branch, making parallel execution safe.

Also reduce stress test iterations (correctness, not throughput):
- TestSameIssueUpdateRace: 10 → 5 goroutines
- TestReadWriteMix: 5x100 → 3x20 readers/writers/iterations
- TestHighContentionStress: 20x50 → 8x15 workers/ops
- TestDeleteIssuesBatchPerformance: 100 → 25 issues

Results: dolt pkg 85s → 25s, migrations 12s → 5s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Executed-By: beads/polecats/quartz
Rig: beads
Role: polecats

files.patch:
@@ -132,12 +132,12 @@ func TestSameIssueUpdateRace(t *testing.T) {
 		t.Fatalf("failed to create issue: %v", err)
 	}
 
-	const numGoroutines = 10
+	const numGoroutines = 5
 	var wg sync.WaitGroup
 	var successCount atomic.Int32
 	var errorCount atomic.Int32
 
-	// Launch 10 goroutines to update the same issue
+	// Launch goroutines to update the same issue
 	for i := 0; i < numGoroutines; i++ {
 		wg.Add(1)
 		go func(n int) {
@@ -208,9 +208,9 @@ func TestReadWriteMix(t *testing.T) {
 		issueIDs[i] = issue.ID
 	}
 
-	const numReaders = 5
-	const numWriters = 5
-	const iterations = 100
+	const numReaders = 3
+	const numWriters = 3
+	const iterations = 20
 
 	var wg sync.WaitGroup
 	var readErrors atomic.Int32
@@ -681,8 +681,8 @@ func TestHighContentionStress(t *testing.T) {
 		}
 	}
 
-	const numWorkers = 20
-	const opsPerWorker = 50
+	const numWorkers = 8
+	const opsPerWorker = 15
 	var wg sync.WaitGroup
 	var totalOps atomic.Int32
 	var failedOps atomic.Int32
